title: श्री गरुड दण्डकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गरुड दण्डकम् ॥

ॐ नमो गरुडाय महाबलाय विष्णुवाहनाय॥

जय गरुड सुवर्णपक्ष विस्तीर्णमहाबल।
नागान्तक विषहर वैनतेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥ १॥

वेदात्मने विहगराजाय सुपर्णाय महात्मने।
विष्णुवाहनरूपाय गरुडाय नमो नमः॥ २॥

तार्क्ष्यं वैनतेयं च खगेश्वरं पन्नगाशनम्।
सुपर्णं विष्णुभक्तं च गरुडं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ३॥

गरुड गरुड सुपर्ण खगपति पक्षिराज
कश्यपनन्दन विनतासुत वाहनेश।
नागारिं नागभयहारिणमात्मभक्तं
सर्वव्याधिविनाशकं प्रणमामि नित्यम्॥ ४॥

पक्षवातेन यस्य त्रैलोक्यं कम्पते सदा।
नखैर्विदारयन्नागान् गरुडं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

अमृतं यः समानीय देवेभ्यो दत्तवान् पुरा।
मातृमोक्षार्थमुद्युक्तो गरुडं शरणं गता॥ ६॥

विषनाशकरं देवं सर्वरोगविनाशनम्।
गरुडं सर्वभूतघ्नं सर्ववैरिविनाशनम्॥ ७॥

कद्रूशापविमोक्तारं विनताहर्षवर्धनम्।
नागलोकभयत्राणं गरुडं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ८॥

विष्णोर्वक्षसि यो नित्यं ध्वजरूपेण राजते।
गरुडं वाहनश्रेष्ठं प्रणमामि मुहुर्मुहुः॥ ९॥

गरुडदण्डकमिदं यः पठेद् भक्तिसंयुतः।
विषभयान्मुच्यते नित्यं सर्पदोषाद् विमुच्यते॥ १०॥

॥ इति श्री गरुड दण्डकम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Garuda Dandakam ||

Om Namo Garudaya Mahabalaya Vishnu-vahanaya ||

Jaya Garuda Suvarna-paksha vistirna-mahabala |
Nagantaka Vishahara Vainateya namo-astu te || 1 ||

Vedatmane Vihagarajaya Suparnaya Mahatmane |
Vishnu-vahana-rupaya Garudaya namo namah || 2 ||

Tarksyam Vainateyam cha Khageshwaram Pannagashanam |
Suparnam Vishnu-bhaktam cha Garudam pranamamy-aham || 3 ||

Garuda Garuda Suparna Khagapati Pakshiraja
Kashyapa-nandana Vinata-suta Vahanesh |
Nagarim Naga-bhaya-harinam-atma-bhaktam
Sarva-vyadhi-vinashakam pranamami nityam || 4 ||

Paksha-vatena yasya trailokyam kampate sada |
Nakhair-vidarayan-nagan Garudam pranamamy-aham || 5 ||

Amritam yah samaniya devebhyo dattavan pura |
Matri-mokshartham-udyukto Garudam sharanam gata || 6 ||

Visha-nashakaram Devam sarva-roga-vinashanam |
Garudam sarva-bhutaghnam sarva-vairi-vinashanam || 7 ||

Kadru-shapa-vimoktaram Vinata-harsha-vardhanam |
Naga-loka-bhaya-tranam Garudam pranamamy-aham || 8 ||

Vishnor-vakshasi yo nityam dhvaja-rupena rajate |
Garudam vahana-shreshtham pranamami muhur-muhuh || 9 ||

Garuda-dandakam-idam yah pathed bhakti-samyutah |
Visha-bhayan-muchyate nityam sarpa-doshad vimuchyate || 10 ||

|| Iti Shri Garuda Dandakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: सुवर्ण पक्ष वाले, महाबली, नागान्तक, विषहर, विनता-पुत्र गरुड को नमस्कार। वेदात्मा, पक्षिराज, सुपर्ण, महात्मा, विष्णुवाहन गरुड को नमो नमः। तार्क्ष्य, वैनतेय, खगेश्वर, पन्नगाशन, विष्णुभक्त गरुड को प्रणाम। कश्यप के पुत्र, विनता के सुत, पक्षिराज, नागभयहारी, सर्वव्याधिनाशक गरुड को नित्य प्रणाम। जिनके पंखों की वायु से त्रिलोक काँपता है, जिन्होंने अमृत लाकर देवताओं को दिया, माता के मोक्ष के लिए उद्यत हुए, विषनाशक, सर्वरोगनाशक, विष्णु के वक्ष पर ध्वजरूप में विराजमान — ऐसे गरुड को बारम्बार प्रणाम।
benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of fear of snakes and serpent afflictions (sarpa dosha)
Neutralizes all types of poison and venom
Traditionally recited for removal of all diseases and ailments
Traditionally invoked for protection from enemies and evil spirits
Invokes the protection of Garuda, Vishnu's divine vehicle
description: Garuda Dandakam (श्री गरुड दण्डकम्) is a devotional poem in Dandaka metre dedicated to Lord Vishnu. This sacred composition is sourced from Garuda Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to invoke the protection of Garuda, Vishnu's divine vehicle, grant courage and fearlessness, and protect from enemies and evil spirits. For best results, devotees chant this on Sunday (Ravivar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Garuda Panchami and Nag Panchami. At 10 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 7 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Garuda Dandakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Garuda Dandakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Garuda Dandakam (श्री गरुड दण्डकम्) is a powerful Sanskrit stotra composed in the Dandaka metre (long-syllabic prose-rhythmic meter) dedicated to Bhagavan Garuda — the divine Suparna (golden-winged) eagle, son of Sage Kashyapa and Vinata, vahana of Bhagavan Vishnu, and the eternal nemesis of nagas (serpents). Traditionally attributed to Swami Vedanta Desika (1268–1369 CE) — the great Sri Vaishnava acharya who composed it during a vision of Garuda at Tirumala — the stotra invokes Garuda through his various epithets (Tarksya, Vainateya, Khageshwara, Pannagashana, Suparna, Pakshiraja) and concludes with the powerful phala-shruti — Visha-bhayan-muchyate nityam sarpa-doshad vimuchyate — the reciter is freed from all fear of poison and serpent-afflictions. The text is preserved in classical Garuda Purana tradition and Sri Vaishnava paddhati. (Source: Garuda Purana; Vedanta Desika tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Garuda and why is He Vishnu's vahana?
seoFaqs.answer: Garuda (गरुड — the devourer) is the divine king of birds (Pakshiraja, Khageshwara), son of Sage Kashyapa-prajapati and his wife Vinata, half-brother of the nagas (born of Kadru, Vinata's co-wife). The Garuda story is detailed in the Mahabharata Adi Parva (Astika Parva): when Vinata was enslaved by Kadru through deceit, the newly-hatched Garuda undertook to bring amrita (nectar of immortality) from svarga to ransom his mother. He defeated all the devas (whose wing-wind shook the three worlds), reached the amrita-kalasha and brought it to earth, but Vishnu intercepted him and granted a boon — Garuda chose to be Vishnu's eternal vahana and to remain higher than Vishnu in posture (atop His flag) but lower in service. Garuda thus became Vishnu's vahana, His dhvaja-rupa (flag-form) and the eternal Naga-antaka (serpent-destroyer). (Sources: Mahabharata Adi Parva; Garuda Purana; Bhagavata Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Garuda Dandakam recited for sarpa-dosha and snake fear?
seoFaqs.answer: Garuda is the cosmic Naga-antaka (नागान्तक — serpent-destroyer) and Vishahara (विषहर — poison-remover) — verse 1 of the Dandakam invokes Him as Nagantaka Vishahara Vainateya. The mythological basis is that nagas are Garuda's cousins-turned-prey through the Kadru-Vinata enmity, and his very presence terrifies all serpentine beings. By extension, in Hindu remedial astrology, Garuda is the cosmic remover of: (1) Sarpa-dosha (afflictions caused by Rahu-Ketu shadow-planets, the cosmic serpents) in the natal horoscope, (2) Kala Sarpa Yoga (when all seven planets are between Rahu-Ketu), (3) Visha-yoga (snake-venom afflictions, both literal and metaphorical relationship-poisons), (4) Naga-shapa (curses from snakes killed in past lives), and (5) Naga-pratishtha-dosha (afflictions from disturbing snake-habitats). The Dandakam is recited at the famous Naga-temples (Mannarsala, Subramanya, Kukke) and at Tirumala for sarpa-dosha pariharam. (Sources: Garuda Purana; classical sarpa-dosha shanti tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Garuda Dandakam be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Garuda Dandakam is most powerful on Sunday (Ravivar — the day of Surya, who is also a Vishnu-related deity) and Saturday (Shanivar — the day of Shani, helpful for sarpa-dosha shanti), Garuda Panchami (Shravan Shukla Panchami — the special day of Garuda worship in South India), Naga Panchami (Shravan Shukla Panchami in some calendars — for snake-related shanti), Tirumala Garuda Seva days (the annual Brahmotsavam at Tirumala when Sri Venkatesha is taken in procession on the Garuda-vahana — September-October), and during a Rahu or Ketu Mahadasha or Antardasha. Devotees of Tirumala recite it before major pilgrimages, those facing snake-related fears or repeated dreams of snakes recite it 41 times daily for 41 days, and Sri Vaishnavas chant it as part of daily Vishnu upasana. (Sources: Garuda Purana; Tirumala paddhati; sarpa-dosha shanti tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Garuda Dandakam grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti verse 10 declares — Garuda-dandakam-idam yah pathed bhakti-samyutah, visha-bhayan-muchyate nityam sarpa-doshad vimuchyate — whoever recites with devotion is daily freed from fear of poison and from sarpa-dosha. Tradition further attributes: (1) protection from snakes, scorpions and venomous creatures both physical and dream-based, (2) cure of all roga (verse 7 — sarva-roga-vinashanam), (3) destruction of enemies (verse 7 — sarva-vairi-vinashanam), (4) pariharam of Kala Sarpa Yoga and Sarpa-dosha in the horoscope, (5) protection during Rahu and Ketu mahadasha periods, (6) courage and fearlessness (verse 4 invokes naga-bhaya-harinam), (7) blessing for Tirumala pilgrimage success (since Garuda is Vishnu's vahana), (8) cure for skin diseases caused by snake-curse, (9) atmabhakti and ascending to Vaikuntha through Vishnu's vahana, and (10) liberation through Garuda's intercession. (Sources: Garuda Dandakam phala-shruti; Garuda Purana; Sri Vaishnava tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Garuda Dandakam differ from other Garuda mantras and Garuda Purana?
seoFaqs.answer: All are Garuda devotional texts but differ in scope. (1) The Garuda Purana (one of the eighteen Mahapuranas, 19,000 verses) is a vast scripture covering creation, preta-kalpa (afterlife teachings recited during 13-day shraddha rituals), dharma-shastra, ayurveda, ratna-shastra (gemology) and karma-vipaka (consequences of deeds) — it is best known for the preta-khanda recited at funeral rites. (2) The Garuda Mantra / Garuda Gayatri (Tat-purushaya vidmahe Suvarna-pakshaya dhimahi tannah Garudah prachodayat) is a Vedic-style Gayatri for daily Garuda meditation. (3) The Garuda Dandakam (10 verses) is the principal Sanskrit stotra dedicated to Garuda — focused on iconographic invocation through 8+ epithets and the powerful sarpa-dosha-removing phala-shruti. Devotees recite the Dandakam for daily upasana, the Gayatri for japa, and the Garuda Purana selectively for specific knowledge. (Sources: Garuda Purana; comparative Garuda stotra tradition.)
